# benchmark.score { #tspaint.benchmark.score }

`tspaint.benchmark.score`

Score benchmark paintings against a truth table — the head-to-head leaderboard (CLAUDE.md §9).

Given a ``truth.npz`` (from :func:`tspaint.benchmark.export.export_vcf` or ``tspaint simulate
--truth``) and one or more tool painting ``.npz`` files (any source that wrote the
``tspaint-painting`` format, including the native painter), reports per tool:

* **balanced accuracy** — class-balanced per-base correctness (``tspaint.validate``);
* **accuracy** — plain span-weighted per-base correctness;
* **confidence** — mean ``|2·P − 1|`` (a calibrated soft caller relaxes toward 0 where it cannot
  tell, so high accuracy with *moderate* confidence is the honest regime; CLAUDE.md §9);
* **switch-density ratio** — inferred ÷ true ancestry switches/length at a confidence ``deadband``
  (1.0 = faithful tract-length distribution for admixture dating; >1 over-fragments).

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [aggregate](#tspaint.benchmark.score.aggregate) | Collect :func:`score_full` JSONs into two tidy CSVs in ``out_dir``. |
| [format_table](#tspaint.benchmark.score.format_table) | Render :func:`score` rows as a fixed-width text table. |
| [load_truth](#tspaint.benchmark.score.load_truth) | Load a ``tspaint-truth`` ``.npz`` into ``{sample: [(left, right, state)]}``. |
| [score](#tspaint.benchmark.score.score) | Score one or more tool paintings against ``truth``. |
| [score_full](#tspaint.benchmark.score.score_full) | Full benchmark metrics for one painting vs ``truth`` — the three families (CLAUDE.md §9). |
| [write_metrics](#tspaint.benchmark.score.write_metrics) | Write a :func:`score_full` result to ``path`` as JSON (NaN → null). |

### aggregate { #tspaint.benchmark.score.aggregate }

```python
tspaint.benchmark.score.aggregate(json_paths, out_dir)
```

Collect :func:`score_full` JSONs into two tidy CSVs in ``out_dir``.

Writes ``summary_scalar.csv`` (one row per painting: scenario ``meta`` columns + painter
``name`` + the scalar metrics) and ``summary_by_size.csv`` (long form: one row per painting ×
true-segment-length bin, with the bin's accuracy / weight / segment count) — ready for plotting
the three metric families across the grid.

Returns the two CSV paths.

### format_table { #tspaint.benchmark.score.format_table }

```python
tspaint.benchmark.score.format_table(rows)
```

Render :func:`score` rows as a fixed-width text table.

### load_truth { #tspaint.benchmark.score.load_truth }

```python
tspaint.benchmark.score.load_truth(path)
```

Load a ``tspaint-truth`` ``.npz`` into ``{sample: [(left, right, state)]}``.

### score { #tspaint.benchmark.score.score }

```python
tspaint.benchmark.score.score(
    truth,
    paintings,
    *,
    deadband=DEFAULT_DEADBAND,
    K=2,
)
```

Score one or more tool paintings against ``truth``.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**truth**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`) or [dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A ``tspaint-truth`` ``.npz`` path or an already-loaded ``{sample: [(l, r, state)]}``.

<code>[**paintings**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [str](`str`)\] or [iterable](`iterable`)\[[str](`str`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Tool paintings: ``{name: npz_path}``, or paths (named by basename).

<code>[**deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [DEFAULT_DEADBAND]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Confidence dead-band for the hard segmentation used in the switch-density ratio (default 0.4; CLAUDE.md §9 — argmax-tspaint over-fragments, a deadband recovers the true tract-length distribution).

<code>[**K**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of ancestry states (default 2).

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[dict](`dict`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   One row per painting with ``name``, ``balanced_accuracy``, ``accuracy``, ``confidence``, ``switch_density_ratio`` and ``n_samples``.

### score_full { #tspaint.benchmark.score.score_full }

```python
tspaint.benchmark.score.score_full(
    truth,
    painting,
    *,
    name='',
    meta=None,
    bins=None,
    deadband=DEFAULT_DEADBAND,
    state=0,
    K=2,
)
```

Full benchmark metrics for one painting vs ``truth`` — the three families (CLAUDE.md §9).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**truth**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`) or [dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A ``tspaint-truth`` ``.npz`` path or a loaded ``{sample: [(l, r, state)]}``.

<code>[**painting**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to a ``tspaint-painting`` ``.npz``.

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [\'\']{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Painter label stored in the result.

<code>[**meta**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Scenario metadata (model, T_split, …) stored verbatim for aggregation.

<code>[**bins**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[array_like](`array_like`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   True-segment-length bin edges (default :data:`tspaint.validate.DEFAULT_SIZE_BINS`).

<code>[**deadband**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [DEFAULT_DEADBAND]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Confidence dead-band for the hard segmentation in the fragmentation metric (default 0.4).

<code>[**state**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Ancestry state whose global proportion is reported (default 0).

<code>[**K**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of ancestry states (default 2).

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``name``, ``meta``, ``n_samples``; **overall proportions** (true/est/error); **accuracy** (balanced/per-base/confidence); **fragmentation** (true & inferred switches per Mb, ratio, breakpoint precision/recall); and **accuracy by true segment size** (``size_edges`` / ``size_accuracy`` / ``size_weight`` / ``size_n_segments``).

### write_metrics { #tspaint.benchmark.score.write_metrics }

```python
tspaint.benchmark.score.write_metrics(path, result)
```

Write a :func:`score_full` result to ``path`` as JSON (NaN → null).
